2606.11018.title: Measuring Human Value Expression in Social Media Texts: Calibrated LLM Annotation and Encoder Transfer
2606.11018.paper_summary: Measuring subjective constructs in naturally occurring social media text requires annotation procedures that are theoretically grounded, empirically validated, and transferable to an encoder model for scalable prediction. Using non-English social media posts annotated according to Schwartz's theory of basic human values, we investigate how different LLMs, prompts, and instruction languages operationalize the expression of values in text. We argue that although texts may permit multiple plausible interpretations, theory-based value definitions can constrain interpretations and reduce spurious value attributions. Beyond precision, recall, and F1, we evaluate structural alignment between values, error structure, confidence-ambiguity relations, and annotation stability. We show that different LLMs produce different value interpretations. Iterative prompt calibration through error analysis reduces misattributions and improves alignment with expert annotations. We also derive targeted expert verification rules from recurrent error structures and use them during corpus annotation. Finally, we show that LLM annotations can be transferred to an encoder model through soft-label training, retaining theory-based value interpretations and information about uncertainty in value expression.
2606.11018.paper_summary_zh: 測量自然發生的社交媒體文本中的主觀構念需要理論基礎、經驗驗證且可轉移到編碼模型的註釋程序，以實現可擴展的預測。<lb/>使用根據施瓦茨的基本人類價值理論標註的非英語社交媒體帖子，我們研究不同的LLM、提示和指令語言如何在文本中操作化價值的表達。<lb/>我們認為，雖然文本可能允許多種合理的解釋，但基於理論的價值定義可以限制解釋並減少虛假的價值歸因。<lb/>除了精確度、召回率和F1，我們還評估價值之間的結構對齊、錯誤結構、信心-模糊關係和註釋穩定性。<lb/>我們顯示不同的LLM產生不同的價值解釋。<lb/>通過錯誤分析的迭代提示校準減少了錯誤歸因，並改善了與專家註釋的對齊。<lb/>我們還從重複的錯誤結構中推導出針對性的專家驗證規則，並在語料庫註釋過程中使用它們。<lb/>最後，我們顯示LLM註釋可以通過軟標籤訓練轉移到編碼模型中，保留基於理論的價值解釋和有關價值表達不確定性的信息。
2606.11018.author: Maria Milkova et.al.
